verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is canonically a type II Golgi-resident transmembrane glycosyltransferase anchored in the trans-Golgi membrane with its catalytic domain facing the Golgi lumen. However, a well-documented plasma membrane form exists: proteolytic cleavage in the stem region (at Arg-77) generates a shed soluble form, but prior to shedding the intact TM-anchored protein traffics to the cell surface where it acts as a cell-cell and cell-matrix recognition molecule (e.g., sperm-egg interactions). The membrane-anchored protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM domain before shedding. This documented, cell-type/state-dependent surface pool qualifies as dual_localization contextual rather than a full 'yes', because the dominant compartment is the Golgi and surface presence is restricted to specific biological contexts.
